text:
is the 13th chapter of the 2nd volume and the 40th chapter overall of the Spoof on Titan manga, written and illustrated by hounori.

Overview

 Both Bertolt and Ymir end up angering Reiner and Krista, respectively. 

Summary

Ymir spots Bertolt all alone and points out that she almost did not recognize him without Reiner by his side. Bertolt denies this, but Ymir continually teases him and asks if they fought, as Bertolt had appeared to be quite gloomy that day. When Bertolt denies a second time, Ymir laughs at how even best friends like them have the ability to fight. Bertolt is disgruntled and tells Ymir that the situation is no laughing matter, making her admit that she had irked Krista, who now refuses to talk to her as well.

Ymir recalls the lengths she went to annoying Krista: in order to see more of her cute reactions, she had repeatedly placed caterpillars on her shoulder until she snapped at the fourth. Bertolt sighs upon hearing the story as Ymir asks him about the fight he had with Reiner. He tells her that he attempted to give Reiner a banana but to no avail. Their conversation then escalated to the both of them fighting for the other's health and benefit.

At the cafeteria, Jean remarks that it is unusual for Reiner to sigh. He asks if anything had happened and Reiner apologizes. He explains that he and Bertolt had an argument and yelled at him. In disbelief, Jean tries to lift the mood by saying that better friendships have arguments quite often, however, as Eren passes by their table, he comments that there are exceptions to that statement.

At the same time, Connie approaches Sasha and apologizes for a mistake he had committed. He makes it up to her by offering her a loaf of bread. Although Sasha cannot seem to remember what Connie is apologizing for, she joyfully accepts his offer and results to constantly pestering him in giving her more loaves.

Later on, Ymir shyly approaches Krista and apologizes for angering her. Krista does the same as she claimed to had gone overboard and unnecessarily said too much. Smiling brightly, she then asks Ymir to eat with her again. Ymir rejoices over seeing her smile and concludes that making her mad was worthwhile.

Back in the cafeteria, Bertolt ruefully thinks of how he can apologize to Reiner. When he decides to consult someone to speak about his problems, he goes to Reiner himself, who rhetorically asks why he came to him for advice. As he and Bertolt meet the Cadets, Jean and Eren cheer them on for making up.

Ymir congratulates Reiner and Bertolt for settling things with each other. Bertolt asks her how things are going with Krista and notices the scribbles she had written down on paper. As he and Reiner become unamused at Ymir's list of ways to anger Krista, Ymir reasons that she is doing as such just to see her smile again.
